extern type Number;
cap Add { fn add(a: Self, b: Self): Self }
extern fn js_add(a: Number, b: Number): Number;
impl Number: Add { fn add(a: Self, b: Self): Self = resume(js_add(a, b)) }

fn one_plus_two(): Number = Add.add(1, 2)
fn main(): Number = one_plus_two()

===EXPECT===
# After LTO: `one_plus_two`'s Perform is resolved via recursive
# monomorphization — the impl method body is inlined directly at the call
# site with params substituted and `resume(v) → v`. With a small body and a
# single caller, heuristic D picks C (call-site inline), so `one_plus_two`
# itself is absorbed into `main`. Key invariants:
#   - the inlined body calls `js_add` directly (no cap bundle threading).
#   - neither `__impl_Number_Add.add` nor `__caps.Add_Number.add` appears in
#     `__main_cps` (the handler-factory wrapper would emit an undefined
#     `.add` property; inlining sidesteps the wrapper entirely).
#   - no `one_plus_two__lto` clone is emitted (inlined, not cloned).
# Follow-up (separate bug): the outer `main()` wrapper still constructs
# `{ Add_Number: __impl_Number_Add(__identity) }` because the post-LTO
# re-typecheck preserves `Infer` caps as a floor. Tracked in
# plans/unify-impl-and-handler.md / lto follow-ups.
js_add(
!__caps.Add_Number.add
!__impl_Number_Add.add
!one_plus_two__lto
